rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO CO.** IN THE MATTER OF THE ROGER W. SLEIGHT 2010 DATED NOVEMBER 18, 2010 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Co-Trustee Marilyn Bessey?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,592 Jointly And Severally Against Backer And His Counsel; Declaration Of James J. Finsten
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Co-Trustee Marilyn Bessey's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Special Interrogatories is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. "Unless otherwise limited by order of the court in accordance with this title, any party may obtain discovery regarding any matter, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action or to the determination of any motion made in that action, if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery or of any other party to the action." (Code Civ. Proc., section 2017.010; see Probate C., section 1000.) In probate proceedings, the court determines the subject matter at issue by reference to the verified petitions, responses and objections. (See Forthmann v. Boyer (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 977, 985.) " ' "[T]he scope of discretion always resides in the particular law being applied, i.e., in the ' "legal principles governing the subject of [the] action....' Action that transgresses the confines of the applicable principles of law is outside the scope of discretion and we call such action an " 'abuse' " of discretion." ' [Citations.]" (Thayer v. Wells Fargo Bank (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 819, 833.) A party may move to compel further responses to interrogatories on the ground that an answer to a particular interrogatory is evasive or incomplete or an objection is without merit or too general. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.300, subds. (a)(1) & (3).) Generally, the party seeking discovery meets his or her burden simply by a fact-specific showing of relevance. (Kirkland v. Superior Court (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98.) Once good cause is shown, the burden shifts to the opposing party to justify his or her objection. (Ibid.) An order granting or denying discovery must be informed by the liberal policies of discovery statutes in mind when reviewing decisions denying or granting discovery. (See Pacific Tel. & Tel. Co. v. Superior Court (1970) 2 Cal.3d 161, 171.) Here, Bessey has made the required showing as to interrogatories 1 and 3, and interrogatory 2 to the extent it seeks information regarding brokerage accounts or vehicles. Such information is relevant to Bessey's duties to marshal and account for trust assets and the account before the court is contested. Backer, for his part, has not justified his objection to these interrogatories. Co-trustee Mark Backer must provide the required responses within 10 days of the date notice of entry of this order is served upon him. Bessey has failed to make the required showing as to interrogatories 4, 5, 7 and 8, in that she has not made a fact-specific showing of relevance of any responses on the following topics: (1) expenditures of trust assets for improvements on the Steiner Street residence during the decedent-settlor's lifetime, when the trust was revocable and prior to Bessey's appointment; or (2) Backer's personal travel in September 2019. Even if Bessey had carried her burden, Backer has justified his privacy objections to these requests. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO CO.** IN THE MATTER OF THE ROGER W. SLEIGHT 2010 DATED NOVEMBER 18, 2010 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Co-Trustee Marilyn Bessey?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,592 Jointly And Severally Against Backer And His Counsel; Declaration Of James J. Finsten - (Added To Calendar For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only) -
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Co-trustee Bessey's request for sanctions is DENIED. Co-trustee Backer's request for sanctions is DENIED. The parties are advised that the court expects more meaningful good faith meet-and-confer by the parties and any future discovery motions must be accompanied by detailed documentation of those efforts. Co-trustee Bessey shall lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, prior to the date of the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on December 10, 2020. Remote appearance is required, whenever possible, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]
